title: Auto-aligning cable sensor
abstract: An auto-aligning cable sensor for performing measurements on a medium contained in a vessel is provided. The cable sensor includes a cable which has an end sensor and which is adapted to be carried by a mounting fixture. The mounting fixture and the vessel are movable into a measurement position relative to each other such that a contact face of the end sensor is held in alignment against a surface of the vessel by a pressing force, thereby enabling the end sensor to sense a characteristic of the medium contained in the vessel. The cable sensor further includes an elastically deformable coupling member which is arranged to mediate transfer of the pressing force from the mounting fixture to the cable and thence to the end sensor when the mounting fixture and the vessel are moved into the measurement position. The coupling member deforms under the pressing force such that the cable can flex to tilt the end sensor relative to the surface of the vessel to accommodate misalignment of the contact face and the surface of the vessel.
claims: Claims 1. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) for performing measurements on a medium contained in a vessel (10), wherein the cable sensor includes: a cable (13) which has an end sensor and which is adapted to be carried by a mounting fixture (11 ), the mounting fixture and the vessel (10) being movable into a measurement position relative to each other such that a contact face (15) of the sensor end is held in alignment against a surface (16) of the vessel by a pressing force, thereby enabling the end sensor to sense a characteristic of the medium contained in the vessel; and an elastically deformable coupling member (17) which is arranged to mediate transfer of the pressing force from the mounting fixture (11 ) to the cable (13) and thence to the end sensor when the mounting fixture and the vessel (10) are moved into the measurement position, the coupling member deforming under the pressing force such that the cable can flex to tilt the end sensor relative to the surface of the vessel to accommodate misalignment of the contact face (15) and the surface (16) of the vessel. 2. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to claim 1 , wherein the elastically deformable coupling member (17) is formed as a sleeve around the cable, an end of the sleeve abutting an abutment surface (18) of the cable (13) to transfer the pressing force to the cable. 3. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to claim 2, wherein the abutment surface (18) is the underside of a flange (19) formed at the end of the cable. 4. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to claim 3, wherein the top side of the flange (19) is flush with the contact face (15). 5. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to claim 3 or 4, wherein the flange (19) is formed by a ferrule (20) at the end of the cable (13). 6. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to any of the previous claims, wherein one of the end of the cable (13) and the surface (16) of the vessel (10) has a protrusion (30) and the other of the end of the cable (13) and the surface (16) of the vessel (10) has matching bay (32) that receives the protrusion when the contact face (15) of the sensor end is held in alignment against the surface (16) of the vessel by the pressing force, thereby preventing sideways motion of the end of the cable relative to the surface (16) of the vessel. 7. An auto-aligning cable sensor (12) according to claim 6, wherein the contact face (15) is located at the protrusion (30) or the matching bay (32) of the end of the cable (13). 8. A measurement apparatus for performing measurements on a medium contained in a vessel (10), the system including: the auto-aligning cable sensor (12) of any one of the previous claims; and a mounting fixture (1 1 ) carrying the cable sensor (12), the mounting fixture and the vessel (10) being movable into a measurement position relative to each other such that the contact face (15) of the end sensor is held in alignment against a surface (16) of the vessel by a pressing force to sense a characteristic of the medium contained in the vessel, and such that the coupling member (17) mediates transfer of the pressing force from the mounting fixture to the cable (13) and thence to the end sensor when the mounting fixture and the vessel are moved into the measurement position. 9. A measurement apparatus according to claim 8, wherein the mounting fixture (1 1 ) is a base for supporting the vessel, the mounting fixture and the vessel (10) being movable into the measurement position by locating the vessel on the base. 10. A measurement apparatus according to claim 8 or 9, wherein the deformation of the coupling member (17) under the pressing force seals, or improves a seal of, the coupling member to the mounting fixture (1 1 ). 11. A measurement apparatus according to any one of claims 8 to 10, wherein the elastically deformable coupling member (17) abuts an abutment surface (21 ) of the mounting fixture (11 ) to transfer the pressing force from the mounting fixture to the coupling member. 12. A measurement apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the cable sensor (12) is threaded through a hole (14) formed in the mounting fixture (11 ), the hole being configured to allow the cable (13) to flex therein to tilt the end sensor relative to the surface (16) of the vessel, and the hole having a shoulder which forms the abutment surface (21 ) of the mounting fixture. 13. A bioreactor system for fermentation or cell culture having one or more bioreactor vessels (10) and one or more respective measurement apparatuses according to any one of claims 8 to 12 for performing measurements on media contained in the, or each, bioreactor vessel. 14. A bioreactor system according to claim 13, further having a robotic manipulator for moving the, or each, bioreactor vessel into the measurement position. 15. A method for performing measurements on a medium contained in a vessel (10), the method including the steps of: providing the measurement apparatus of any one of claims 8 to 12; moving the mounting fixture (1 1 ) and a vessel (10) into a measurement position relative to each other such that the contact face (15) of the end sensor is held in alignment against a surface (16) of the vessel by a pressing force, and such that the coupling member (17) mediates transfer of the pressing force from the mounting fixture to the cable (13) and thence to the end sensor, the coupling member deforming under the pressing force such that the cable can flex to tilt the end sensor relative to the surface of the vessel to accommodate misalignment of the contact face and the surface of the vessel; and using the aligned contact face (15) to sense a characteristic of the medium contained in the vessel (10).
